explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to **references to future or subsequent events in a sequence** (such as upcoming projects, scheduled plans, successors, or next stages in a process), often signaled by words like "next," "following," "will be," or temporal phrases indicating imminent or later timeframes.

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears across diverse domains including business planning, sports tournaments, political transitions, entertainment releases, and public policy timelines. It activates in multiple languages (English, German, Russian, Danish) and text genres like news articles, quotes, and announcements, consistently focusing on forward-looking statements about what follows the current moment.

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: High-activation texts explicitly mention sequential progression or future-oriented actions (e.g., "next round," "next year’s budget," "moving on to"), while low-activation examples describe isolated present or past events (e.g., a shooting, a fire, a lawsuit) without referencing subsequent steps or timelines.

4. **Closing interpretation**: The neuron detects linguistic markers of temporal succession and planning, essentially functioning as a "what comes next" identifier in narratives and reports. It ignores static descriptions or events lacking explicit forward momentum.

results.actual_text: we managed the release of restrictions on access to funds by the federal government, the next step is to identify all the personnel that will join our team to continue the reconstruction
results.plain_text: Housing Dept. seeks to fill 200+ jobs for recovery work  Share  The Puerto Rico Housing Department is looking to fill more than 200 jobs available under recovery programs created with Community Development Block Grant (CDBG-DR) Program funds.  “As part of our commitment to expedite the island’s recovery process, we’re seeking to accelerate the recruitment of professionals in specialized areas,” Housing Secretary William Rodríguez said, adding that a job fair is planned for May 7-14 as part of the recruitment process.  “After we managed the release of restrictions on access to funds by the federal government, the next step is to identify all the personnel that will join our team to continue the reconstruction of Puerto Rico,” he said.  “We’re confident that the more than 200 vacancies will be filled by Puerto Ricans eager to work for our island,” said Rodríguez.  The opportunities available to specialized professionals span the fields of procurement, architecture, environmental science, purchasing, communications, finance, engineering, legal, planning, health, secretarial, occupational security, and information systems, among others.  To conduct the job fair, the Housing Department designed an appointment system that will follow health recommendations to guarantee social distancing and avoid excessive crowding, he said. People who register will be cited to the Housing Department’s Barbosa Ave. headquarters for an initial interview with personnel from each area.  To validate their appointments, those interested must send their resume to the link provided in a confirmation email that they will receive upon registering.  The agency also created a LinkedIn page through which publications will be posted to promote the job fair and from where more information can be obtained.  Lastly, Rodríguez said that as part of compliance with the administrative process for the recruitment of new personnel, the agency will only consider those who meet the requirements of the position, evidenced by the original professional and academic certifications, added to the work and study experience required by each position.

predictions.text: “After we managed the release of restrictions on access to funds by the federal government, the next step is to identify all the personnel that will join our team to continue the reconstruction of Puerto Rico,” he said.
predictions.reasoning: This section explicitly mentions the next step in a sequence, using 'next step' and 'will join', which matches the neuron's focus on future events.

results.actual_text: peace in Northern Island, was buried Thursday in his hometown of Derry — the next stop on his itinerary being Hell. If McGuinness went to his grave believing
results.plain_text: Former IRA commander Martin McGuinness, the sectarian devil who later helped orchestrate peace in Northern Island, was buried Thursday in his hometown of Derry — the next stop on his itinerary being Hell.  If McGuinness went to his grave believing in the Catholicism in which he was strictly raised, there is no other destination for a man who plotted the deaths of so many during the infamous Irish Troubles.  He won’t be alone, of course. There were scores of murderous devils on both sides of the sectarian divide, with few hardcore Catholic republicans or Protestant loyalists less bloody than the other.  Rebel to the end, McGuinness’ coffin was draped in the tricolor flag of Ireland, but lacked the overt display of his paramilitary past such as the placing of a beret and black gloves atop the casket.  But their presence can be imagined nonetheless.  During the 20th anniversary of the Troubles in the late 1980s, which I covered extensively as Sun Media’s European bureau chief, Martin McGuinness was omnipresent at rallies in Belfast and Londonderry — Derry, to Catholics — and loudly promoting Northern Ireland’s independence from Great Britain at “any cost.”  He was there, as was I, when two off-duty British soldiers were ambushed and lynched after accidentally being boxed in by an IRA funeral cortege in West Belfast—hauled from their car, stripped and then shot in the head.  These were very bloody days in a long “war” that cost some 3,600 lives, a thousand of which were British security personnel.  And Martin McGuinness, his paramilitary background as boss of the IRA’s Derry brigade softened by a senior position with Sinn Fein, the IRA’s political wing, was always front and centre.  It has been written that a seismic moment in McGuinness’ conversion to peace beginning in the 1990s was when Queen Elizabeth offered him her hand to shake during the 2012 Diamond Jubilee visit to Belfast.  McGuinness, then deputy first minister in Northern Ireland’s peace alliance, had been the provisional IRA’s face back in 1979 when the Queen’s cousin, Lord Mountbatten, also uncle to Prince Philip, was blown up by an IRA bomb planted on his boat while he was on holiday off the coast of Ireland.  This took courage on the Queen’s part to offer her hand to someone she no doubt despised, but she did so for the sake of peace.  While he did not bow before her as protocol demands, McGuinness did shake her hand for similar reasons, and out of respect for her courage to even look him in the eye.  He wished her goodbye and Godspeed — in Irish.  The double entendre, however, did not go unnoticed.  James Martin Pacelli McGuinness — his unusual third name in honour of Pope Pius VII — was born as second of seven children to a religious Catholic family who shared two bedrooms and an outdoor toilet in the crowded slum in Derry known as the Bogside.  He died Tuesday at age 66 of a rare genetic heart disorder.  On Thursday, thousands lined the streets to bid farewell to Martin McGuinness, even those who had wished him an earlier death for the atrocities he had invariably planned during the height of the Troubles.  His role in Northern Ireland’s peace process is what brought these people out, including international dignitaries and former U.S. President Bill Clinton whose years in office saw the transformation of a brutal sectarian war into a still-tentative peace.  “I came to treasure every encounter with Martin McGuinness,” said Clinton following Mass. “If you want to continue his legacy, go and finish the work he has started.”  In other words, give this devil his due.

predictions.text: Former IRA commander Martin McGuinness, the sectarian devil who later helped orchestrate peace in Northern Island, was buried Thursday in his hometown of Derry — the next stop on his itinerary being Hell.
predictions.reasoning: It contains 'next stop', a direct reference to a subsequent event in a sequence, which strongly matches the neuron's explanation.

predictions.text: “I came to treasure every encounter with Martin McGuinness,” said Clinton following Mass. “If you want to continue his legacy, go and finish the work he has started.”
predictions.reasoning: This section urges future action with 'finish the work', indicating subsequent steps, aligning with the neuron's focus on what comes next.

predictions.text: It has been written that a seismic moment in McGuinness’ conversion to peace beginning in the 1990s was when Queen Elizabeth offered him her hand to shake during the 2012 Diamond Jubilee visit to Belfast.
predictions.reasoning: It references the beginning of a peace process, implying a sequence of events, which may activate the neuron's detection of subsequent stages.

results.actual_text: an understanding that the practical phase of putting these retaliatory measures into effect will begin next week," Zakharova was quoted as saying by state news agency Tass.
results.plain_text: The chief editor of Kremlin-funded satellite TV channel RT says the network will accede to a U.S. demand to register as a foreign agent but also intends to challenge the order in court. RT reported Thursday that the U.S. Department of Justice has demanded the registration be done by Monday. The Justice Department declined to comment to The Associated Press. Russia's Foreign Ministry spokeswoman Maria Zakharova said Russia would retaliate, but did not elaborate. "There is an understanding that the practical phase of putting these retaliatory measures into effect will begin next week," Zakharova was quoted as saying by state news agency Tass. RT Editor-in-Chief Margarita Simonyan said a failure to register could mean the arrest of the channel's American director and the freezing of its accounts. Simonyan said on RT's Russian-language website that "between such consequences and registration as a foreign agent, we are forced to choose registration. Although, of course, we categorically disagree with this requirement." "We believe this requirement is not just contrary to the law, and we intend to prove it in court. This requirement is discriminatory, it contradicts both the principles of democracy and freedom of speech," she said. RT began operations in 2005 under the brand Russia Today, claiming to present the Russian view on global news and to cover stories ignored by mainstream Western news media. It has been widely criticized in the West as a propaganda outlet and for trafficking in conspiracy theories. Amid continued controversy about Russia's role in trying to influence last year's U.S. presidential election, Twitter last month banned advertising by RT and by the state-funded Sputnik news agency. pushed it to spend millions on advertising ahead of the 2016 presidential election. In a Senate Judiciary Committee hearing last month, Sen. Dianne Feinstein (D-Calif.) asked why YouTube gave RT a preferred status on the video site, and kept it for so long. Richard Salgado, director for law enforcement and information security at Google, said the site's preferred status was dropped because of declining viewership. Russia's embassy in the U.S. denounced the reported Justice Department order. "The brutal pressure on the Russian media proves that the American side is still following the path of conscious deterioration of relations," the embassy said in a statement on Facebook. "We consider its requirements as a desire to eliminate the source of alternative information, an unacceptable violation of international standards of press freedom." The move comes as the House and Senate intelligence committees, and Special Counsel Robert Mueller, investigate Russian meddling in the 2016 election and any ties to Trump associates.

predictions.text: “There is an understanding that the practical phase of putting these retaliatory measures into effect will begin next week,” Zakharova was quoted as saying by state news agency Tass.
predictions.reasoning: This section explicitly states 'will begin next week', a clear future event, matching the neuron's response to temporal phrases.

predictions.text: RT Editor-in-Chief Margarita Simonyan said a failure to register could mean the arrest of the channel's American director and the freezing of its accounts.
predictions.reasoning: It describes possible future consequences with 'could mean', indicating subsequent events, which fits the neuron's pattern.

predictions.text: “We believe this requirement is not just contrary to the law, and we intend to prove it in court. This requirement is discriminatory, it contradicts both the principles of democracy and freedom of speech,” she said.
predictions.reasoning: This section mentions 'intend to prove it in court', pointing to future actions, aligning with the neuron's focus on forward-looking statements.

results.actual_text: Theo ghi nhận của chúng tôi, trong sáng 17.1,
results.plain_text: Theo ghi nhận của chúng tôi, trong sáng 17.1, trên QL1, đoạn qua Khu công nghiệp Tân Tạo (P.Tân Tạo A, Q.Bình Tân) có rất nhiều mảnh đinh hình thoi nằm khắp mặt đường ở làn xe máy. Đa phần các mảnh đinh này còn rất mới.  Nhận tin báo, Công ty cổ phần đầu tư phát triển hạ tầng IDICO, đơn vị quản lý tuyến đường trên, cử công nhân thu gom, hút đinh để đảm bảo việc đi lại an toàn cho người dân.  Chỉ trong thời gian ngắn, các công nhân đã hút được cả trăm mảnh đinh hình thoi cùng nhiều vụn sắt trên đoạn đường dài chừng 100 m.  Theo người dân, trước đó 1 ngày đã phát hiện các mảnh đinh hình thoi rải trên đường. Có người chạy xe máy cán đinh, thủng lốp, phải dẫn bộ, người dân ra nhặt đinh đem đi xử lý. Sáng nay 17.1, tình trạng rải đinh tiếp tục xuất hiện, người dân báo cho đơn vị quản lý tuyến đường cử người xuống thu gom, hút đinh.  “Hôm qua tôi nhặt được cả nắm đinh. Loại đinh này cán là loạng choạng tay lái liền, nguy hiểm lắm”, anh V. (người dân sống gần khu vực trên) bức xúc.  Các công nhân thu gom đinh cho biết, ngày lễ, nhất là những ngày cận tết, nhu cầu đi lại người dân tăng cao, tình trạng rải đinh tại đây xuất hiện nhiều hơn và liên tục, gây nguy hiểm cho người tham gia giao thông.  Tiếp nhận thông tin từ chúng tôi về việc rải đinh trên QL1, lãnh đạo UBND P.Tân Tạo A (Q.Bình Tân) cho biết, sẽ cho người kiểm tra, nếu phát hiện sẽ xử lý nghiêm.  Tin liên quan  Khổ sở vì hàng trăm xe máy, ô tô bị cán đinh trên Quốc lộ 51 TP.HCM: Người đàn ông 16 năm thầm lặng hút đinh trên QL1 TP.HCM: Kiên quyết xử lý nghiêm nạn rải đinh tái diễn trên cầu vượt Tỉnh lộ 10

results.plain_text:
Related Phys.org National University of Singapore scientists have fabricated nano-sized vesicles from cells via a cell shearing approach that can be used for various biomedical applications. Extracellular vesicles (EVs) are naturally occurring cell-secreted lipid-bound membrane vesicles found in all biological fluids. They have gained much attention for their potential role in several biomedical applications including diagnostic purposes (biomarkers), as therapeutic agents and as drug delivery systems. The use of EVs as therapeutic agents, particularly those obtained from stem cells, has shown regenerative and/or tissue reparative effects. It's been a goal of researchers to grow retinal cells outside the body and use them to replace dead or dysfunctional tissues inside the eye.
